races.race_name: carpetgallop.co.uk Where Performance Begins "National Hunt" Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Red Oak
runners.spotlight: Musselburgh maiden hurdle winner (2m4f, soft) who bounced back from being pulled up in a Grade 2 at Kelso when a respectable fourth of nine on handicap debut at Newbury (2m3f, good to soft) last month; it's possible this drop back in trip will be a positive and he's a key player.
runners.comment: His handicap debut fourth at Newbury last month reads very well in today's context

runners.name: Blues Singer
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (good to soft) who had contested some warm races over hurdles prior to winning a big-field maiden at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month with something to spare; Alan King has a 42% strike-rate at Hexham; leading claims under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won big-field maiden at Huntingdon last month and holds leading claims under a 7lb penalty

runners.name: Imperfectlyperfect
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner (good to soft) who followed up on hurdle debut over C&D (good) last October; the return to Hexham and drop back in trip could suit following two heavy defeats over 2m5f/2m6f, but a couple of the other previous winners have shown stronger form.
runners.comment: C&D winner who could be suited by the drop back in trip but needs some improvement

runners.name: Hurlyburly
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of his first two bumper runs (good to soft/soft) when 7l second at Haydock (1m7f, good to soft) last month; this half-brother to the useful jumper Arvico Bleu brings potential to today's hurdle debut and he's an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up in third and final bumper and he's an each-way contender on hurdle debut

runners.name: Marvel Magic
runners.spotlight: Close fourth on debut in bumper at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in November and he should get back on track at some point for his good yard; however, well beaten on his last two starts, including over hurdles.
runners.comment: Close fourth on bumper debut but well beaten on last two starts, including over hurdles

runners.name: Countryman Joe
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big odds in both bumpers and pulled up when 300-1 on recent hurdle debut here (2m4f, soft); a tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Tailed off at big odds in both bumpers & pulled up when 300-1 on recent hurdle debut here

runners.name: C'Mon So
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up who is with a respected stable but he hasn't shown enough across his three rules starts to suggest he'll be threatening today.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown enough across his three rules starts to suggest he'll be threatening today

runners.name: Prince Phil
runners.spotlight: Fifth of nine, beaten just over 10l, on debut in bumper at Kelso (2m, heavy) in February; the run gave him something to build on but he was pulled up at 150-1 in a maiden hurdle at Wetherby (2m, good) last month.
runners.comment: Displayed ability on bumper debut at Kelso but pulled up at 150-1 on sole hurdle start

runners.name: Pimmsoclock
runners.spotlight: Showed a good attitude to win on his debut in France (2m2f, very soft) last October; he's changed hands since and this half-brother to the useful Cherie d'Am looks a bright prospect; not ruled out on British debut.
runners.comment: Won his sole start in France and looks a bright prospect; not ruled out on British debut

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in points and beaten 27l at triple-figure odds last month on his first two starts over hurdles (2m, soft/good).
runners.comment: 0-7 in points and beaten 27l at big odds last month on his first two starts over hurdles

runners.name: Caspada
runners.spotlight: His bumper debut at Sedgefield (2m1f, soft) in December wasn't devoid of promise but he's finished down the field on both starts since, most recently on hurdle debut at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Bumper debut wasn't devoid of promise but down the field on both starts since

runners.name: Mademewhatiam
runners.spotlight: 7yo who is a longstanding maiden and it will be a surprise if he gets anywhere close to getting off the mark today.
runners.comment: 7yo who is a longstanding maiden and would be a shock winner today

races.race_name: Anthony Woods Electrical Lightning Bolt Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Heads Or Harps
runners.spotlight: His best form has come on soft/heavy ground which is unlikely to prevail today and he's failed to threaten the winner when second at Ayr (2m4f, soft) on his last two starts; however, those were still positive performances and he may benefit from the drop back in trip; not discounted.
runners.comment: 2nd on last two starts, albeit no match for the winner; may benefit from this shorter trip

runners.name: Powerofjet
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (2m, good to soft) last November on second chase start and some good runs since, most recently 12l third of 12 in series final at Carlisle (2m, good to soft); this is less competitive; high on the list.
runners.comment: 3rd in series final at Carlisle last month and this is less competitive; high on the list

runners.name: Royal Deeside
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 2m4f here (good) last year and he's run well in third in 2m4f contests at Carlisle (good to soft) and Haydock (good) on his last two starts; has been shaping as though this drop back in trip could be a positive and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 2-time course winner who has been shaping as though this drop back in trip could be a plus

runners.name: Kicksaftersix
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row over 3m here last spring (good/good to soft); returned from an absence with encouraging sixth over hurdles at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) recently; however, he may well find today's trip inadequate.
runners.comment: Hat-trick of wins over 3m here last spring and he may well find today's trip inadequate

runners.name: That One
runners.spotlight: Won the novice hurdle (2m, good) on this card 12 months ago; he needs to improve upon what he produced in two handicap hurdles in February but the return to Hexham could help and he might be suited by the switch to fences.
runners.comment: Needs to better February hurdle form but this course winner isn't ruled out on chase debut

runners.name: Too Cool Forshrule
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in series final at Carlisle (2m, good to soft) three weeks ago; however, he's won twice this season (2m4f/2m5f, soft), the latest two starts ago; won on good ground last term and there can be hope that he's still okay at this sort of trip; in the mix if swiftly bouncing back.
runners.comment: Pulled up three weeks ago but two wins this season and in the mix if bouncing back

runners.name: Flash Du Pistolet
runners.spotlight: Mixed form this term but when soundly beaten at Carlisle last time he was in a competitive series final; now just 2lb higher than when readily completing a double at Kelso (2m1f, good to soft) last March so can go well if back on song; tongue-tie added to cheekpieces adopted last time.
runners.comment: Mixed form this season but on a handy mark and can go well if back on song

runners.name: Young Getaway
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last February and went close in a novice hurdle here (2m, good) next time; makes chase/handicap debut following a year off but with his trainer in phenomenal form; could have a big part to play, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Makes chase/handicap debut following a year off but with trainer in phenomenal form

runners.name: Rock On Jet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was beaten 9l when last of the three finishers at Ayr (2m, soft) in January; however, he's been dropped a handy 3lb since and is capable of better judged on last season's evidence; not written off.
runners.comment: Not at top of his game on his two runs this season but on a reduced mark; not written off

runners.name: Elusive Warrior
runners.spotlight: Poor form in bumpers and over hurdles, and his jumping wasn't great when third on recent chase debut at Sedgefield (2m, good); from out of the handicap he's 4lb higher than last time but there was some promise in that performance and he's open to further improvement if finding greater fluency.
runners.comment: Some promise in his chase debut and he's open to improvement if finding greater fluency

runners.name: Choose A Copper
runners.spotlight: There was no return to form on his two starts this winter; arrives with plenty to prove but he's down to a dangerous mark and, if he is to stage a revival, it's likely to be here where he's posted both victories.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove but if he is to stage a revival, this is the likeliest venue

runners.name: Nonbinding
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning two novice hurdles for Gordon Elliott in autumn 2022; however, he's hard to fancy on what he's shown subsequently.
runners.comment: Two novice hurdle wins in autumn 2022 but hard to fancy on subsequent evidence

races.race_name: Horslyx-For Horse's Health & Welfare Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: My My My Delilah
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in bumpers last season but is 0-6 over hurdles this term; drops back in trip after 37l defeat at Sedgefield (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month; considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles, having struggled in all three handicaps

runners.name: She's Notjoeking
runners.spotlight: Won from the front at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft; front pair clear) in February and was third under much more patient ride at Ayr (2m, soft) last month; may not want the ground to dry out here but otherwise she's likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Won from the front two starts ago and was third under much more patient ride last time

runners.name: Gintime
runners.spotlight: The form of her small-field maiden win in December doesn't amount to much but she bettered her low-key handicap debut when creditable fifth of 15 in valuable series final at Kelso (2m, good to soft) last month; commands respect in this easier race.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth in valuable series final at Kelso last month; commands respect here

runners.name: Out On Her Own
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but runner-up twice at Carlisle (2m1f, soft/2m3f, good to soft) since returning from a break last month and has another big role to play here.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but runner-up twice at Carlisle since returning from a break last month

runners.name: Rose's Dart
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in first two handicaps (2m4f, soft/heavy) but combination of better ground and first-time tongue-tie enabled significant improvement at Market Rasen (2m2f, good to soft) last month, where she kept on too strongly for the odds-on favourite; further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Came good when back on better ground and when tongue tied last month; still lightly raced

runners.name: Snapaudaciaheros
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in any of her three 2m4f starts this year but has yet to be placed after eight hurdle runs; drops back in trip with cheekpieces tried here; improvement required.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight hurdle runs; back in trip with cheekpieces added today

runners.name: Dee's Getaway
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to cause 50-1 shock over C&D (soft; front pair clear) last month and is now 3-13 over hurdles; it's hard to predict whether she'll be in the right mood again but she won't mind if the ground dries out.
runners.comment: Shock C&D winner last month and now 3-13 over hurdles; contender if in right mood again

runners.name: Theirshegoes
runners.spotlight: Out of form towards end of last year but returned from three-month break with pretty good fourth in first-time cheekpieces at Kelso (2m5f, good) 11 days ago, and it will be interesting to see whether this drop back in trip pays dividends.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good form this month, after a break; drops back in trip today

races.race_name: Mick Walsh Open Ditch Handicap Chase

runners.name: Jamesieconn
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won his last three starts (2m7f-3m2f, good to soft/good), the latest at Carlisle nine days ago; clearly thriving and could be tough to beat for a stable that is in fantastic form.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has won his last three starts and could be tough to beat

runners.name: I See The Sea
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this season and well beaten at Bangor a fortnight ago; however, he won off this mark at Catterick (3m1f, good) in November and was a good second over C&D (good to soft) last March; shouldn't be written off.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and well beaten a fortnight ago but on last winning mark and not written off

runners.name: King Of Cong
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over fences and he's struggled on his last two starts; however, first-time cheekpieces had a positive impact when he was second off 4lb higher over C&D (good to soft) last November, so perhaps today's first-time blinkers and tongue-tie will give him a boost.
runners.comment: He's struggled on his last 2 runs but perhaps the change of headgear will give him a boost

runners.name: Dr Shirocco
runners.spotlight: 11yo whose losing run goes back 11 races to November 2024 but he ran pretty well off his lower hurdle mark at Musselburgh last month, following a break, and all four of his wins have come over fences (two of them here); not discounted back chasing.
runners.comment: Ran pretty well off lower hurdle mark last month; all four wins have come over fences

runners.name: Eveque
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a win over C&D (good to soft) last November and he's back down to the same mark for his first visit here since; however, this 12yo has struggled subsequently and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Won off this mark over C&D on reappearance but this 12yo has struggled subsequently

runners.name: Lewa House
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off 3lb higher over C&D (good to soft) last March but unable to recapture that form across his five starts this season; wears first-time tongue-tie/visor (replacing cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Runner-up off 3lb higher over C&D last March but unable to recapture that form this season

runners.name: Streamsforth Lad
runners.spotlight: He's fallen/unseated on three of his five starts over hurdles but was going well when exiting two out at Stratford (2m6f, soft) last month and ran well when runner-up to well-backed winner at Bangor (3m, good) a fortnight ago; major player granted a clear round.
runners.comment: Runner-up to well-backed winner at Bangor two weeks ago; major player granted clear round

runners.name: Joie More
runners.spotlight: Respectable fifth of 14 at Carlisle (3m, good to soft) in February but well beaten at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) last month; has had a second wind op since, which needs to make a difference if he's to break his duck.
runners.comment: Maiden who was well beaten at Newcastle last time; wind op needs to have made a difference

runners.name: Euchan Falls
runners.spotlight: 0-18 over fences and hasn't been shaping of late as though a win is imminent; 10lb out of the handicap today and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-18 over fences and down the field in this sphere on his last two runs; 10lb wrong today

runners.name: Gibberwell
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have come here (including this race in 2023) and he was 8lb out of the handicap when back here last time; however, this 10yo has been pulled up on his last three starts and needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Last two wins have come here but this 10yo has been pulled up on his last three starts

runners.name: Muroor
runners.spotlight: Beaten just a neck over C&D last May but hasn't threatened in four subsequent starts and this 13yo is winless since 2020; 7lb wrong today.
runners.comment: Beaten just a neck over C&D last May but hasn't threatened in his four subsequent starts

runners.name: Rightbackatyou
runners.spotlight: Bumped into progressive winner when runner-up over C&D (soft) last month on his second chase start but beaten 31l and only two completed, so it wasn't a good advertisement for his claims this afternoon.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D last month but beaten 31l and only two completed

runners.name: Blue Indigo
runners.spotlight: Mistakes eventually caught up with him when he fell three out on last month's chase debut over C&D (soft); however, he was in second at the time and could play a leading role if first-time cheekpieces help his jumping.
runners.comment: In second when falling four out over C&D on chase debut; cheekpieces could help jumping

runners.name: Reliance Kid
runners.spotlight: Went close in a point in May 2024 but he's struggled under rules and was well beaten at 28-1 on his recent chase debut over C&D.
runners.comment: He's struggled under rules and was well beaten at 28-1 on his recent chase debut over C&D

runners.name: Redeeming Love
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward in a first-time tongue-tie (retained) when third at Southwell (3m, good) three weeks ago on her handicap hurdle debut; finished third in her sole point and her dam was a useful chaser, so this very lightly raced 6yo could have more to offer now switched to fences.
runners.comment: Third on handicap hurdle debut and she could have more to offer now switched to fences
runners.owner: R & W, The 8 Amigos, Burns, Austerfield

runners.name: Jet Light
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in seventh in three maiden hurdles and pulled up on handicap hurdle debut at Perth last April (3m, good to soft; 12-1); absent since and plenty to prove but this very lightly raced point runner-up is one to watch in the betting now tackling fences in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Achieved little over hurdles; point runner-up; one to watch in the betting on chase debut

races.race_name: Happy Retirement Maurice & Anne Barnes Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Divilabother
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut over C&D (good) last August and good second at Kelso (2m5f, good) next time; tailed off over C&D in October before respectable fourth of 13 at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in December; the closer the ground is to being good, the better; could be a contender after a break.
runners.comment: C&D winner on stable debut last August; could be a contender after a break

runners.name: Asa
runners.spotlight: Close third over hurdles at Wetherby (3m, soft) in January then won over fences at Catterick (3m1f, soft); ran pretty well when 5l third of three in Sedgefield chase (3m2f, good) last time; arrives in good heart and there can be optimism that she'll be fine now back down in trip; possible player.
runners.comment: Has been in good heart over hurdles and fences this year and she's a possible player

runners.name: Balking
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 8yo who was 25-1 runner-up on handicap debut at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) last January; soundly beaten at Ayr this February on sole run since but may have needed that outing; not discounted.
runners.comment: 25-1 runner-up on h'cap debut last January; may have needed this season's sole run since

runners.name: Party In The Park
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 13l when seventh of nine on handicap debut at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in February; however, this 5yo showed promise previously and his pedigree hints that this step up in trip will be a plus.
runners.comment: Pedigree hints that this step up in trip will be a plus on his second handicap start

runners.name: Getaway Master
runners.spotlight: Won at Carlisle (2m3f, soft) in November on second stable start and, with this hood back on, took a step back in the right direction when 7l sixth of 14 over this C&D (soft) last month; showed promise on good to yielding ground earlier in career; capable of building on that latest effort.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when sixth of 14 over C&D; capable of building on that effort

runners.name: Do Ye Wanna
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when third on handicap debut at Carlisle (2m3f, soft) in February; only seventh of 14 over this C&D (soft) since but this lightly raced 8yo isn't written off in view of his penultimate effort.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut in February; underwhelming since but not written off

runners.name: Rock Dj
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up who has run pretty well in defeat on his first two handicap starts (2m4f/2m5f, soft/good to soft); drops back down in grade today and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: He's run pretty well in his first two handicaps and drops back down in grade today
runners.owner: Ursa Major Ltd, Dj Goodman & Partner

runners.name: Stylish Recruit
runners.spotlight: Very hard to fancy on what he's shown over fences/hurdles since last October; however, he's tumbled down the weights and the two best RPRs in his career have been achieved at Hexham, so no surprise to see a good run.
runners.comment: Very hard to fancy on his form since last October but the return to Hexham could help

runners.name: Eire Street
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row in February 2024 and very well treated on that form, but pulled up/tailed off on his last three starts; a visor goes on.
runners.comment: Very well treated on old form but pulled up/tailed off on his last three starts

runners.name: Woodview Lad
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability in Ireland last spring and this very lightly raced 5yo is one to watch in the betting; however, he was tailed off at big odds last month in his first two handicaps.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced but tailed off at big odds last month in his first two handicaps

races.race_name: Happy Retirement Maurice & Anne Barnes Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Kool Raoul
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice under Gregor Walkingshaw in the autumn, including over C&D; however, no impact in his three handicaps; now 4lb lower than his opening mark, Walkingshaw (also on board penultimate start) returns and headgear enlisted.
runners.comment: No impact in his three handicaps; now 4lb lower than his opening mark; headgear enlisted

runners.name: Be The Difference
runners.spotlight: Completed soft-ground hat-trick in November/December (3m, 2m7f and 2m5f) but subsequent evidence suggests this 10yo has gone off the boil; made bad mistake at Ayr last time (two months ago) and pulled up.
runners.comment: Soft-ground hat-trick in November/December but has gone off the boil; bad mistake latest

runners.name: Highbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Chepstow (2m3f, soft) in January; inconsistent, however, and she was tailed off when tried over 3m at Doncaster on latest outing, despite the stamina in her family; connections turn to 7lb claimer.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Chepstow (2m3f, soft) in January; inconsistent, tailed off on latest start

runners.name: Wise Move
runners.spotlight: Four-time point winner who's been thereabouts in a couple of his handicaps this season, most recently when second at Wetherby (2m4f, soft) on Boxing Day; not so good on his next two outings and ran poorly in a maiden on latest, so needs to stop the rot.
runners.comment: Second on Boxing Day (2m4f, soft); not so good on next two outings and ran poorly latest

runners.name: Eric Carmen
runners.spotlight: In cracking form this season, with three wins and a second, until however he went down by 25l at Bangor (good) on latest start; maybe the ground that day was not soft enough; major player if bouncing straight back.
runners.comment: In cracking form until 25l defeat on latest start; maybe good ground was not soft enough

runners.name: Gardener
runners.spotlight: Five races for his new trainer and back on track, following three third places with a tenacious C&D win (soft) under Charlie Maggs in 14-runner race 19 days ago; back up 4lb but one to consider seriously.
runners.comment: Three 3rds before a tenacious C&D win (soft) in 14-runner race last time; back up 4lb
runners.owner: Mrs J M Plummer & M E Sowersby

runners.name: My Boy Christy
runners.spotlight: One bit of ability (in cheekpieces) from four starts in Ireland; another in the first (2m4f, soft) of two handicap runs for this stable, which still demands big overall improvement but cheekpieces are now restored.
runners.comment: Big overall improvement is required but return of cheekpieces offers a little hope

runners.name: Tantivy Vavoom
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated for first in an Irish maiden point last April (3m, good); beaten at least 20l in her three hurdle races, all at about 2m4f, and needs big improvement but handicap debut means she requires a second look.
runners.comment: Needs big improvement but handicap debut means she requires a second look

runners.name: Blended Stealth
runners.spotlight: 1-39 over hurdles, the win gained here (2m, good) last May; inconsistent and although he stays 2m4f and latest start was one of his better days, today's position 7lb out of the handicap makes things tough.
runners.comment: 1-39 over hurdles and today's position from 7lb out of the handicap makes things tough
runners.owner: D Mullen & Exors Of G Barker

runners.name: Our Laura B
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2023 but, following wind surgery, she's not been far away in two of her last three starts; the latest was her fifth of 14 to Gardener over C&D; place chance.
runners.comment: No win since May 2023 but, following a wind op, not far away in two of last three starts

races.race_name: Pointing Pointers Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Nassalam
runners.spotlight: Pulled up/tailed off on all seven starts since winning the Welsh National for the Moore stable in December 2023; it's tricky to assess what he's capable of nowadays but this race is vastly easier than he's used to and if he retains any spark he'll be tough to beat on this stable debut.
runners.comment: 2023 Welsh National winner; he's very much struggled since but this is vastly easier

runners.name: Brandy McQueen
runners.spotlight: Quite useful in his prime and he won a point in February (3m, good to soft) before easily following up in a hunter chase at Catterick last month (3m1f, good; Lunar Contact second); strong claims.
runners.comment: Easily won a hunter chase at Catterick last month and holds strong claims

runners.name: Potters Party
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful chase form for Henry de Bromhead as recently as winter 2024-25; he's finished in the first three in two points this year and could be involved returning to rules action.
runners.comment: Fairly useful in winter 2024-25; could be involved returning to rules action

runners.name: Whatdeawant
runners.spotlight: Third in Grade 1 novice hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival in March 2022 for Willie Mullins; he's raced in points on his last seven starts, winning five of them; one to be interested in now back under rules.
runners.comment: Plenty of back-class; good strike-rate in points more recently; one to be interested in

runners.name: Settle Down Jill
runners.spotlight: Won mares' novice hurdle for Dan Skelton last October and ran respectably in second in a hunter chase at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft) last month on her second start for this yard; shaped last time as though this trip may be within range and she could be involved.
runners.comment: Kept on for second in 2m5f hunter chase at Carlisle and she could be involved

runners.name: Lunar Contact
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner who was a 9l second in this race 12 months ago (good); gets a pull in the weights with the winner Brandy McQueen from their clash at Catterick (3m1f, good) last month but he was soundly beaten in second and has work to do today.
runners.comment: 9l second in this last year; work to do with Brandy McQueen on their clash last month

runners.name: Dressedtoimpress
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 10yo who won the Heart Of All England Maiden Hunters' Chase over C&D (good) in May 2024; however, others have stronger form and this is his first outing since.
runners.comment: Won maiden hunter chase over C&D in May 2024 but this is his first outing since

runners.name: Ex S'Elance
runners.spotlight: Course winner who was third in a point-to-point in January; however, this 12yo has been pulled up off a basement mark in handicaps on his last two starts and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Pulled up off a basement mark in handicaps on his last two starts; others preferred

races.race_name: carpetgallop.co.uk Pro Fibre Surface Setting Standards Open NH Flat Race (Cat 1 Elim) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cricket Hill Denis
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on last month's debut at Wetherby and probably flattered by proximity when fourth of five in slowly run race there ten days later; others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Probably flattered by proximity when fourth of five in slowly run race last month

runners.name: Ar Ais Aris
runners.spotlight: Out of a dual bumper winner; bought for £60,000 after finishing second on Irish point debut in November (yielding ground), form that was boosted when the third won a bumper last month and ran well at Aintree on Saturday; must be considered.
runners.comment: Bought for £60,000 after promising Irish point debut in November; must be considered

runners.name: Gaelic Heather
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare who was placed in a bumper; bought for £50,000 after promising Irish point debut just over a year ago (yielding to soft) and it will be interesting to see how she fares on rules debut.
runners.comment: Bought for £50,000 after promising Irish point debut just over a year ago; rules debut

runners.name: Western Games
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam runner-up in 2m5f-3m hurdles, closely related to useful bumper and jumps winner The Cockney Mackem; market helpful.
runners.comment: Out of close relative of useful bumper and jumps winner The Cockney Mackem; market helpful

runners.name: Moorhead
runners.spotlight: Well clear of the third when second of seven in good-ground point bumper six weeks ago, after which he changed hands for £24,000; new stable has fair bumper strike-rate; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Well clear of the third when second of seven in good-ground point bumper six weeks ago
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock And Paul Drury

runners.name: Captain Barbossa
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to bumper winner Cut The Rope; dam unplaced 6f 2yo (RPR 67), half-sister to a 6f winner; stable has some success in bumpers; market support will bring him into the equation.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a bumper winner; stable has bumper winners; market helpful

runners.name: Dan's Allstar
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement when placed in soft-ground Irish point in January on third start, after which he was bought for £20,000; new trainer has modest strike-rate in bumpers; might be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Bought for £20,000 after Irish point third in January; stable quiet in bumpers

runners.name: Blue Skies Ahead
runners.spotlight: From a good jumping family and she won a good-ground point bumper on debut just over a year ago; made low-key rules debut on soft last month (tongue tied) but might be seen in a better light if the ground dries out.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper in 2025; may low-key rules debut last month but the ground was soft
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs "Confined" Novice Stakes (Not Run More Than Three Times) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crimson Rose
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Godolphin filly; promise at Chelmsford (6f, AW) last June and much better to make short work of her rivals in a maiden at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) three weeks later; absent since and it wasn't a strong field last time but she still looks a leading contender.
runners.comment: Made short work of her rivals at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last July on second start

runners.name: Nevasca Cinza
runners.spotlight: Backed into 9-4 from 20-1 for debut at Carlisle (6f, good) and recovered from a slow start to be a close third; made most when beaten a head in five-runner maiden at Hamilton (6f, soft) later in September; left the Russell/Scudamore stable for 100,000gns in October (gelded since) and should be open to plenty more improvement; player.
runners.comment: Placed both starts; left the Russell/Scudamore stable for 100,000gns in October; player

runners.name: Westport
runners.spotlight: 270,000gns yearling by Blue Point; 6-4 favourite for nine-runner novice at Lingfield (6f, AW) 19 days ago and found his stride nicely for a comfortable win; penalised and needs to build on that display but he's promising.
runners.comment: 6-4 favourite at Lingfield (6f, AW) recently, finding stride nicely for comfortable win
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Aigeas
runners.spotlight: Won debut at Deauville (6.5f, AW) in October but put firmly in his place in French Listed race (7f, heavy) 17 days later; drops back to a novice race for this return and should still bring potential, but he's not alone in that.
runners.comment: Won at Deauville (6.5f, AW) but put firmly in his place in French Listed race soon after

runners.name: Railwayman
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling by Blue Point; fourth foal; half-brother to three winners including Oxted (5f-7f including Group 1 King's Stand and July Cup; RPR 123) and Chipstead (5f-6f; 110); dam 5f/6f winner (96); tongue tied for debut but, representing such strong connections, he is very much one to note.
runners.comment: 420,000gns Blue Point yearling; half-brother to three winners including sprint star Oxted
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Dreamadera
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; sister to Spanish 5.5f/6f winner Antonella (RPR 105), half-sister to five winners including Excellent Times (6f/7f; 97) and Times Warrior (French 7f/1m; 97); dam 5.5f winner (76) from useful sprinting family; market can guide on debut but she's probably one for later on.
runners.comment: From a useful family; market can guide on debut but she's probably one for later on

runners.name: Blazing Sam
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Al Kazeem half-brother to winners Lady Erimus (1m 2yo; RPR 61) and Haughmond (Italian 6f-1m1f); unraced dam closely related to Hong Kong 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Blazing Speed; on debut, probably up against it.
runners.comment: Al Kazeem half-brother to two winners; on debut, probably up against it

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: By Territories; eighth foal; brother to 7f/1m winner With Love (including 2yo/US turf; RPR 87), half-brother to winners The Third Star (7f AW; 87) and Youmzain Star (French 7f/1m1f 2yo; 80); dam French 1m winner; trainer (runs two) was off the mark for 2026 late last month.
runners.comment: By Territories; brother to 7f/1m winner With Love (including 2yo/US turf; RPR 87)

runners.name: Romeo's Guardian
runners.spotlight: Slowly away when towards rear in two 6f maidens on AW last month; that suggests strongly that he's a longer-term project.
runners.comment: Slowly away when towards rear in two 6f maidens on AW last month

runners.name: Squashy Berry
runners.spotlight: By Territories; first foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 79), half-sister to two fairly useful winners, out of Group-placed 5f 2yo winner; the same connections also run Percy's Star, who might be the more likely candidate.
runners.comment: By Territories; 1st foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 79), half-sister to two fairly useful winners

runners.name: Wild Act
runners.spotlight: Hooded but raced freely on both starts (7f/6f, AW) for Charlie Appleby in December, making most for an improved effort when third of six at Lingfield; picked up for 14,000gns in February; others have markedly better form.
runners.comment: 14,000gns buy in February after two AW runs for C Appleby; others have much better form

runners.name: Sun Of Dolly
runners.spotlight: Beat one at Newbury (6.5f, soft) last October; better when sixth of nine at Doncaster (6f, heavy) two weeks later but still needs huge improvement.
runners.comment: Better on second start in the mud last autumn but still needs huge improvement

runners.name: Moonlight Angel
runners.spotlight: 28-1, weakened into a well-beaten last of seven in maiden at Ayr (6f, good) last June and reportedly finished lame; has left Mick Appleby.
runners.comment: 28-1, reportedly finished lame when last at Ayr (6f, good) last June; has left M Appleby

races.race_name: Best Ticket Deals Online At leicester-racecourse.co.uk Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Court Alert
runners.spotlight: Second in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August is the best form in this field; it's also, however, easily his best form (four races) after he trailed the field in a much stronger Newmarket sales race (7f, good) in October; gelded since and has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: 2nd in 6f Newmarket sales race last August is the best form in this, easily his best too

runners.name: Beagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Shaped with a lot of promise when beaten a head at Newbury (1m, good) last April; not seen again until last month but duly went one better when 1-2 at Chelmsford (7f, AW); carried his head high but brings plenty of promise.
runners.comment: Stacks of promise last April; went one better when 1-2 at Chelmsford (7f, AW) last month

runners.name: King's Cavalry
runners.spotlight: Lots to like on pedigree and he came from off the pace in promising fashion when sixth of 15 in novice at Newmarket (7f, good) in October; that was at 50-1 but he's probably a leading player this time.
runners.comment: Lots to like on pedigree; promising from off the pace at Newmarket (7f, good) in October

runners.name: Erudition
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Mehmas half-brother to winners Straight Answer (6f/8.5f including Listed/US; RPR 112) and Utile (1m3f AW; 89); dam Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (105), half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Announce; sold by Juddmonte for 26,000gns this February; it will be interesting to see what the betting market makes of him.
runners.comment: By Mehmas; sold by Juddmonte for 26,000gns this February

runners.name: Flor De Guerra
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling by Mehmas; first foal; dam French maiden, half-sister to winners Altea (US 8.5f stakes) and Aarhus (useful French/Spanish 7.5f 2yo/1m), out of Spanish 5.5f/6f 2yo winner; from stable which has had few runners this year; market may guide.
runners.comment: 55,000euros yearling by Mehmas; stable has had few runners this year

runners.name: Golden Harvest
runners.spotlight: 13,000gns yearling by Washington DC; fifth foal; brother to Italian 6f-8.5f winner Doze, half-brother to 5f/6f winner Rocket Money (RPR 78); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (70) out of 7.4f Group 3 winner; probably one for later.
runners.comment: 13,000gns yearling by Washington DC; probably one for later

runners.name: Landslide
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; Territories half-brother to 6f-8.6f winner Voltaic (RPR 79); dam unplaced at 1m1f in France (54), half-sister to 11.6f Group 3 winner Saddlers Quest and two Listed winners at 1m4f+; others look more likely on paper.
runners.comment: By Territories; makes his debut and others look more likely on paper

runners.name: I'll Be Back
runners.spotlight: By Lope De Vega out of a French 5.5f Listed winner (RPR 97); 10-11 for debut in a nine-runner maiden at Doncaster (6f, heavy) in November and rewarded the support, always prominent; this is likely to be run on very different ground but he brings plenty of potential.
runners.comment: 10-11 winner on debut in a nine-runner maiden at Doncaster (6f, heavy) in November

runners.name: Dumuji
runners.spotlight: 16-1 for a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) in November but always prominent and went on over 2f out for a half-length win, finding more when seriously challenged; that's not the best form in this line-up but he should improve.
runners.comment: 16-1 at Kempton (7f, AW) in November but always prominent for a half-length win

runners.name: Society Charm
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and 125-1 when slowly away and beaten about 11l in two 7f events on AW recently; needs a transformation.
runners.comment: 33-1 and 125-1 when slowly away and beaten about 11l in two 7f events on AW recently

runners.name: Mertoun
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling; low-level form at Salisbury (7f, good) and Newbury (6.5f, soft; raced freely up front) in October but it's notable that he was a well-backed favourite in the former and just 13-2 in the latter; gelded since.
runners.comment: Low-level form both starts in October but there was plenty of market interest; gelded

runners.name: Clough
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery before debut; 33-1, no show when last of eight at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) last Tuesday; looks bred for a fair bit further.
runners.comment: 33-1, no show when last of eight at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) last Tuesday
runners.owner: Matt Broughton, Chris Hughes & D Cunha

races.race_name: Monday 25th May "Picnic At The Races" Bank Holiday Handicap

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: Mostly ran with credit last term and did so again when third of 15 at Doncaster (1m2f) on 2025 reappearance; this step back up in trip should suit (1m6f winner last October); strong candidate.
runners.comment: This step back up in trip should suit (1m6f winner last October); strong candidate

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins over about 1m4f last May-August and she scored again at this trip, leading over 1f out and hanging on by a short head, at Lingfield in February after nearly six months off; with another creditable show last time, she looks sure to be bang there again.
runners.comment: Three turf wins (about 1m4f) last May-August; another on AW this year; probably bang there

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won off 1lb lower during a solid sequence last summer/autumn; recent reappearance was respectable, so he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Solid sequence last summer/autumn; recent reappearance was respectable

runners.name: Seraphic
runners.spotlight: Won at Brighton (1m2f) on almost this day last year and has scored twice (1m2f/1m3f) on AW in 2026, including at Kempton on latest start; also effective at 1m4f and well capable of another bold show.
runners.comment: Won on turf on almost this day last year and has scored twice (1m2f/1m3f) on AW in 2026

runners.name: Serviceman
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in two maidens (1m/1m2f) in autumn 2024 for Owen Burrows; absent 489 days, gelded and changed stables before his last of eight in a novice at Kempton (1m4f; 16-1) 19 days ago, which is not the strongest recommendation but he could easily have needed that run, has better chance going handicapping and wears first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Absent 489 days before recent return; better chance going handicapping; first headgear

runners.name: Alta Rica
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden after eight races; beaten a neck in first-time cheekpieces at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in January but ran poorly next time (cheekpieces) and moderately on latest (visor); well treated on best efforts but others inspire a bit more confidence.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden; well treated on best efforts but others inspire a bit more confidence

runners.name: Johnny Boom
runners.spotlight: 28-1 winner over C&D (soft) in October and returned to that form when second at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) last Tuesday; his fast-ground win was in 2021 but he's clearly a major player if this is one of his good days.
runners.comment: C&D win (soft) in October; back-to-form second at Pontefract (good to soft) last Tuesday

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Savannah Smiles
runners.spotlight: Turf wins have come on stiff tracks, at 6f and 5f; had another good season in 2025 but it ended quietly in the autumn and she now has a hood refitted for the first time in over two years; has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Had a good season in 2025 but quiet end to the year and hood refitted on comeback

runners.name: Just Queen High
runners.spotlight: Two wins from last three 6f runs, most significantly at Nottingham (heavy; has won on good; first-time blinkers) in October; faded quickly on recent 7f return at Doncaster and this trip should suit better; easing ground would suit.
runners.comment: Two wins from last three 6f starts, latest on heavy; better for recent return over 7f

runners.name: Spring Bloom
runners.spotlight: Winless since two 6f successes at Newmarket (good) in August 2024 but he ran some good races in defeat last year and is quite temptingly treated on debut for an in-form yard; has often gone well on his seasonal return and may well do so again.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 but ran well in defeat last year; well treated for in-form new yard

runners.name: Lodge
runners.spotlight: Emphatic winner of uncompetitive 6f maiden at Ffos Las (good) on debut in August; creditable show in her first handicap, over 7f at Wolverhampton in January, but she dropped out quickly over 1f out at Southwell since; this trip looks her best option and it's too soon to question her.
runners.comment: Won 6f maiden on debut; stays 7f but tame effort over that trip latest; now back at 6f

runners.name: Liosa
runners.spotlight: Winning reappearance on AW in 2025 and there's other evidence that shows he goes well fresh; however, he's 0-9 on turf and is hard to weigh up, having left Stuart Kittow since some so-so runs in the autumn.
runners.comment: Goes well fresh and won on comeback (AW) in 2025; 0-9 on turf and now with new trainer

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Creditable show on Newcastle AW latest following a break, considering best form is on turf; generally versatile but this stiff C&D suited when he shook off his rivals in October (good to soft; has won on good to firm) on sole course start; has share of weight but should go well.
runners.comment: Creditable AW return; C&D winner in October on sole course start; can give good account

runners.name: Thunderous Love
runners.spotlight: Went from strength to strength in 2025, her five 6f wins culminating with one at Salisbury (good to firm, as for the other turf successes) last July; made winning comeback last year but that was off a lowly mark; needs to be right on her game to win this reappearance.
runners.comment: Five 6f wins in 2025, going right up weights; needs to be on her game to win today

runners.name: Waistcoat
runners.spotlight: Ran flat at Kempton in January when the race was said to have come too soon following previous  Chelmsford win; that win took his strike-rate to 4-6 in 6f handicaps, one over C&D (good) last April; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Race came too soon latest but previously 4-6 in 6f handicaps, including one over C&D

runners.name: Al Barez
runners.spotlight: Losing turf run goes back four years and he wasn't particularly convincing on grass in the middle months of 2025; however, he is 11lb lower than the latest attempt and his recent AW run at Kempton was quite encouraging; revival on the cards with Rossa Ryan up for the first time.
runners.comment: Latest turf win in 2022; fair AW run latest and now 11lb lower than latest run on grass

runners.name: Bay Breeze
runners.spotlight: Back on much the same mark as his latest win, an amateur jockeys' race at Carlisle (good to soft) last May; has won on reappearance but he tends to need the run these days.
runners.comment: Rain welcomed and has a weights chance but he tends to need the run these days

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: Wins have come on the AW and he boasts a patchy record on turf (0-6); turned in a couple of fair efforts in defeat, latterly behind Em Four, since finding a Lingfield handicap panning out ideally off 2lb lower in the winter.
runners.comment: Wins have come on the AW and he boasts a patchy record on turf (0-6); needs more

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Two wins during a consistent spell on the AW this year and these undulations shouldn't pose a problem as a previous Chepstow winner; helped set the race up for the closers at Kempton last time and that effort can be upgraded a touch; player.
runners.comment: Helped set the race up for the closers last time and that can be upgraded a touch; player

runners.name: Al Hofzan
runners.spotlight: Couple of decent efforts under aggressive Harry Vigors rides in 7.2f Wolverhampton handicaps since the cheekpieces went on; first crack at sprinting on his turf debut and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces have helped; first crack at sprinting on his turf debut and is one to consider

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: Stable yet to get rolling for the year but started favourite on his Doncaster comeback recently; said to have stopped quickly there and goes in first-time headgear today; softer ground would have been ideal back to sprinting (last autumn's win came over 1m).
runners.comment: Doncaster flop; softer ground would have been ideal back to sprinting; bit to prove

runners.name: Tenbob Tommy
runners.spotlight: First crack at sprinting when winning a Polytrack maiden three months ago (effort well advertised since); stiffer 6f should suit on his return to turf (stays 1m) but it's doubtful he's suddenly improved and he's back to handicap company on a career-high mark.
runners.comment: Stiffer 6f should suit but he's back to handicap company on a career-high mark

runners.name: The Coffee Pod
runners.spotlight: Picked up for 4,000gns from the Hannon yard last autumn after breaking a blood-vessel for the second time in four runs; not much luck and maybe fresh is the time to catch him, going in a first-time hood, but the stiff 6f is a concern (most starts at 5f).
runners.comment: Maybe fresh is the time to catch him (has bled), in a hood, but the stiff 6f is a concern

runners.name: Magic Music
runners.spotlight: Looked flattered from the front when third in a weak maiden last August, and otherwise didn't achieve much; only run over 7f to date and needs to have improved during her seven months off.
runners.comment: Only run over 7f to date and needs to have improved during her seven months off

runners.name: Rogue Allegiance
runners.spotlight: Promise when winning on his Tapeta debut as a 2yo but struggled badly last year; often been inclined to pull hard and maybe the hood will help, but he ran poorly in it once before; now has first run for Jack Jones (runs in the same colours).
runners.comment: Promising 2yo debut (6f, AW) but struggled last year; hood given second try; stable debut

runners.name: Beyond Borders
runners.spotlight: Four wins for Karl Burke as a 2yo but wildly inconsistent since; back to the mark off which he won easily from the front in a small field last summer but there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh.
runners.comment: Four wins for Karl Burke as a 2yo but wildly inconsistent since; ordinary record fresh

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Today Handicap

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner in 2025; never able to land a blow having been hampered at the start when sixth of ten in 7f AW Lingfield handicap 24 days ago; can bounce back from a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Hampered start when sixth at Lingfield 24 days ago; can bounce back from slipping mark

runners.name: Forever My Prince
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with C&D claiming win in October for Eve Johnson Houghton; not discredited for his new yard after five months off when fifth of 15 in 7f Doncaster handicap 15 days ago; needs considering.
runners.comment: Solid fifth for new yard in 7f Doncaster h'cap latest; C&D winner needs considering

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence and he failed to beat a rival on his return in 16-runner handicap at Doncaster (7f) 15 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: On a losing sequence and came in last on his return at Doncaster (7f) 15 days ago

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: It's now 14 starts since his last success but he comes here in excellent nick, ending up with lot to do when third of nine in 1m Pontefract handicap six days ago; a player off the same mark for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: In excellent nick; ended up with lot to do when Pontefract third six days ago; big player

runners.name: Red Mirage
runners.spotlight: Resumed from four months off with a good third of 15 in 7f Doncaster handicap 15 days ago; must enter calculations off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good 3rd in 7f Doncaster handicap 2 weeks ago after 4-month break; must enter calculations

runners.name: Albeseeingyer
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who shaped as if in need of the run on her return when tenth of 15 in 7f Doncaster handicap 15 days ago; can take a step forward off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; tenth of 15 on her return at Doncaster 15 days ago; can take step forward

runners.name: Brunel Nation
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and he beat only two home in a 7f Lingfield handicap 17 days ago; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 and beat only two at Lingfield 17 days ago; cheekpieces reached for now
runners.owner: R Lane, L Watson & J Langridge

races.race_name: Leicester Races Saturday 25th April Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Gaelic Approach
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form after a five-month absence (gelded) when runner-up in 1m4f Wolverhampton handicap four weeks ago; up 2lb but he's well in the mix.
runners.comment: Advanced his form when runner-up at Wolverhampton latest; up 2lb but well in the mix
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.name: Emma's Letter
runners.spotlight: Bagged 1m Newcastle novice in November and has continued in good form in handicaps, not beaten far when last of four at Southwell (1m) in December; goes over a much longer trip today though and in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: In good form, not beaten far when last of four at Southwell latest; tries much longer trip

runners.name: Engross
runners.spotlight: Off four months/gelded before taking a big step forward on his handicap debut when scoring at Chelmsford (1m2f) 18 days ago; got up late there and this son of Gleneagles has more still to offer now upped in trip; leading claims on his turf debut despite 4lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Big step forward when scoring at Chelmsford; more to offer upped in trip; leading claims

runners.name: Hengist Pod
runners.spotlight: No progress for his first go in handicap company when sixth of seven at Wolverhampton (1m4f) last month; needs to step forward on turf debut.
runners.comment: Only sixth of seven at Wolverhampton last month; needs to step forward on his turf debut

runners.name: Five Moons
runners.spotlight: Shown fair form in her four runs to date, not disgraced when fourth of five on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f) four weeks ago; no forlorn hope on her first go on turf.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fourth at Wolverhampton latest; no forlorn hope on first go on turf

races.race_name: Kube - Leicester's Premier Events Centre Handicap

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but she comes here on the back of a good fourth of 12 in 7f Southwell handicap 18 days ago; not out of things eased 1lb.
runners.comment: 0-10 but good fourth in Southwell handicap 18 days ago; not out of things eased 1lb

runners.name: Space Moon
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on her handicap debut when fifth of nine at Newcastle (1m) 23 days ago; not ruled out on her first run on turf.
runners.comment: Step forward when fifth at Newcastle 23 days ago; not out of things on  first run on turf

runners.name: Euphonia
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but she wasn't disgraced when sixth of nine in 6f Kempton handicap last month; possibilities off a falling mark.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when sixth in Kempton handicap last month; possibilities off a falling mark

runners.name: Starakova
runners.spotlight: Got back on track on her return when fifth of 11 in 7f Chelmsford handicap last month; no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Back on track when fifth at Chelmsford last month; no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark
runners.owner: Howard Bolly Sutton Molony Weller Cowles

runners.name: Monk's Hill
runners.spotlight: Arrives on the back of a good second of ten in 7f Newcastle handicap 25 days ago; yard is going well so she's a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good second at Newcastle 25 days ago; yard going well so she's a player off the same mark
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame in six AW runs, coming in last of eight in a 1m Southwell handicap in January; a major step forward is needed on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Yet to make the frame in six AW runs; plenty more is required on her turf debut

runners.name: Diamond Ali
runners.spotlight: Best effort when runner-up in nursery over C&D in October but he failed to fire in two subsequent AW runs to end 2025; needs to bounce back after a break.
runners.comment: Good runner-up over C&D but failed to fire in two subsequent AW runs; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Calabrian Soldato
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a good second in 5f Chelmsford nursery but he's failed to fire in three runs this year, last of six in 5f Newcastle handicap in February; hood is added after a break.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with good Chelmsford second but he's failed to fire in three runs this year

runners.name: Nando's Pride
runners.spotlight: Little promise shown in a handful of AW runs, only eighth of 11 in 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap last month; tried in different headgear here on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Little promise shown in a handful of AW runs; tried in different headgear for turf debut
runners.owner: S & A Mares And Gentech Products

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f) in September but she failed to kick on, tried visored when beating one home in an 8.6f handicap back there in December on final run for Jack Morland; more needed for her new stable.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Wolverhampton but she failed to kick on; more needed for her new stable

runners.name: Shimmering Spirit
runners.spotlight: Shown just modest form in three Newcastle novices (from 6f-1m) this year; needs this switch to handicaps on turf to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Modest form in three Newcastle novices; needs switch to h'caps on turf to spark progress

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when third at Newcastle (7f) on his return but never able to get in a blow when ninth at Southwell (7f) 18 days ago; he's worth another chance to gain a maiden success.
runners.comment: Shaped well when third at Newcastle but ninth at Southwell since; worth another chance

runners.name: Hurricane Ann
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on her handicap debut when fifth at Kempton (6f) in March but raced wide when only seventh at Chelmsford (6f) 18 days ago; not written off.
runners.comment: Good fifth at Kempton but raced wide when seventh at Chelmsford since; not written off

runners.name: Fullstop
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile maiden; tongue tied and went freely when ninth of ten on his handicap debut at Kempton (6f) 12 days ago; hood also goes on now.
runners.comment: Too free when ninth of ten on handicap debut at Kempton (6f) 12 days ago; hood goes on now

runners.name: Creciente
runners.spotlight: Capable of fair form but ended 2025 with a pair of poor efforts in nurseries at Newbury and Bath in the autumn; needs a big step forward on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a pair of poor efforts in nurseries at Newbury and Bath in the autumn

runners.name: Give Me Sun
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a trio of 7f maidens at 2yrs; more is required after six months off on her first go in handicap company.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in a trio of 7f maidens at 2yrs; more required on first go in h'cap company
runners.owner: Mrs Denis Haynes & Mrs Rebecca Smith

runners.name: Castlekeely
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when sixth in 6f Goodwood novice on his final run for Eve Johnson Houghton; since gelded and it would come as no surprise to see him take a big step forward now going handicapping for James Owen; well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Modest for former yard; no surprise to see him now go forward now handicapping for J Owen

races.race_name: Racing TV Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Breezethroughlife
runners.spotlight: Won small-field course maiden (2m4f, good) on stable debut in October and was creditable second on handicap debut at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in December; big player if judged on those exploits but struggled on last two outings, and much depends on the success of last month's wind op.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut; struggled on next two outings; had wind op last month

runners.name: Raby Mere
runners.spotlight: Returned to peak form in refitted cheekpieces at Southwell (3m, good) three weeks ago, his third start for Caroline Fryer; recent 4lb rise does not look excessive and he also has a course win on his CV; respected.
runners.comment: Back in prime form at Southwell last week and also has a course win on his CV; respected
runners.owner: C J Underwood And Caroline Fryer

runners.name: The Wise Traveller
runners.spotlight: Made all to cause 25-1 surprise at Plumpton (3m1f, good) in September, after a break, but generally disappointing since; pulled up when back at Plumpton a week ago, and can't be supported with much confidence here.
runners.comment: Generally disappointing since Plumpton win in September; pulled up there last week

runners.name: Tropical Speed
runners.spotlight: Consistent and progressive 7yo who made all to score by 6l at Lingfield (2m7f, good to soft) on latest outing; has fitness to prove after 137-day break but could plausibly be seen to even better effect on the forecast faster ground here.
runners.comment: Off since making all at Lingfield in November but this faster ground may suit even better

runners.name: Ilitch
runners.spotlight: Flopped badly when favourite at Warwick last month (bled from the nose) but was one of two to pull clear of the others when second at Doncaster (3m, soft) in January and will be suited by the forecast faster ground here; can feature if back on song.
runners.comment: Flopped badly last time (bled); contender if judged on previous Doncaster second

runners.name: Mahon Falls
runners.spotlight: Placed in three Irish maiden hurdles (2m1f-2m5f) for Willie Mullins in the autumn but probably didn't achieve much when remote third of four on stable debut a fortnight ago (2m3f); up in trip for this handicap debut; needs that recent outing to have brought her on.
runners.comment: Didn't achieve much when remote third of four on recent stable debut; handicap debut today
runners.owner: E W Lewis & Katy Price Racing

runners.name: Alan Bresil
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but wasn't beaten that far when fifth of eight at Ascot (2m7f, good) a fortnight ago and this race looks less competitive; could have a say.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles but not beaten that far at Ascot recently and this race looks easier

runners.name: Rialannah
runners.spotlight: 0-15 over hurdles but posted some good efforts for the Joel Parkinson-Sue Smith stable last term and ran a big race when fifth on recent seasonal/stable debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft); will be suited by this move back up in trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran big race when fifth on recent stable debut and this longer trip will suit; shortlisted

races.race_name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Fortunefavorsdbold
runners.spotlight: Suffered early mishap three starts ago but placed on other four outings this season, most recently when second ay Exeter (3m, good) three weeks ago; ran poorly on only previous visit to Fakenham and would appeal more on ground slower than good but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Placed on four of her five outings this season and features prominently in calculations

runners.name: Annie Nail
runners.spotlight: Won over fences here (2m5f, good) last spring and doubled her course tally in modest handicap hurdle (2m7f, good) a week ago; far too inconsistent to rely upon heavily but reverts to chasing on a good mark and has obvious claims if in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Not very consistent but won over hurdles off today's mark a week ago

runners.name: Ballygelly
runners.spotlight: Beat 1-9 favourite when winning two-runner edition of this race a year ago (good ground) and was in front before last-fence unseat over C&D (good to soft) in October; returned from break with low-key run last month but remains lightly raced for her age, and her course form is a selling point.
runners.comment: Well beaten last month, after a break, but ran well over C&D twice last year

runners.name: Moviddy
runners.spotlight: Arrested her decline when placed in valuable final of this mares' series here (2m5f, good to soft; refitted cheekpieces) in January; two lesser efforts have followed and she's unreliable nowadays but she could be too good for this field if perked up by a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Regressive and inconsistent now but ran well in valuable series final here in January
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate & Miss J Wignall

runners.name: Gilbertina
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing at Southwell 12 days ago and now 0-6 over fences but her close third at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) was a pretty good effort and she has possibilities here if stamina holds out on first venture beyond about 2m5f.
runners.comment: Below par this month but was close third over 2m4f two runs ago; tries a new trip here

races.race_name: Woodfordes Mares' Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Natus Vincere
runners.spotlight: Came good under rules at Huntingdon (2m3f, heavy) in February, her second handicap; found one just too strong when odds-on to follow up at Market Rasen (2m2f, good to soft) last month but was given a wind op soon afterwards and now moves up in trip; probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Off the mark over 2m3f in February; up in trip today; probably still has potential

runners.name: Dartmouth Rose
runners.spotlight: Probably showed career-best form when scoring in first-time cheekpieces at Southwell (2m4f, good) last month; 7lb rise asks quite a lot of her but this C&D also suits and the headgear may prompt further improvement; big player.
runners.comment: Career-best form when scoring in new cheekpieces last month; this C&D also suits

runners.name: Honey I'm Good
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on 2024 hurdle/chase form and shaped with a fair but more promise than eventual 40l defeat might suggest at Sedgefield (3m3f, good to soft) in February, after a long absence; ought to be better suited by the forecast faster ground here; interesting.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old form and shaped quite well in February, after a long layoff

runners.name: Silver Gale
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds in her three qualifying races (2m-2m3f) but fared much better when third of seven on recent handicap debut at Warwick (3m2f, good); that was a modest contest but she remains lightly raced.
runners.comment: Improved form when upped to 3m2f for recent handicap debut, finishing third

races.race_name: Woodfordes Supports Dereham Meeting Point Handicap Chase

runners.name: Prince Cleni
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in February, after a layoff, but looked set to finish second before last-fence mishap at Hereford (3m1f, good) last month, and this move back up in trip will probably suit (placed over 3m3f last summer).
runners.comment: Set to finish second before last-fence mishap at Hereford last month; considered

runners.name: Esperti
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old but won three chases last year (3m-3m2f) and looked back in good nick when keeping-on second at Chepstow (3m6f, good to soft) last month; still unexposed over marathon trips and Sean Bowen is booked again; respected.
runners.comment: Back in good nick when keeping-on second at Chepstow (3m6f) last month; respected

runners.name: Blackacre
runners.spotlight: Won twice in a visor at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good/good to soft) last summer; untested beyond that trip and absent since respectable second of three in the autumn (without headgear, as today) but current mark is workable if he's fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Dual winner last summer; absent since the autumn but resumes on a workable mark

runners.name: Slaney Opera
runners.spotlight: Former pointer/hunter chaser who won Sedgefield handicap (3m2f, good to soft) for Ruth Jefferson in January and signed off for her with clear second at Wetherby (3m, good) last month; upped in trip and fitted with cheekpieces for this stable debut; likely contender.
runners.comment: Won at Sedgefield in January and ran well in defeat last month; now with new stable

races.race_name: Weatherbys Design & Print Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jamada
runners.spotlight: Irish bumper winner who has finished second on two of his four hurdling starts for Olly Murphy, latterly in 18-runner Lingfield maiden (2m, soft) in January; below that form on heavy ground in February but this much quicker surface ought to suit him better; good chance.
runners.comment: Second in 18-runner maiden in January and had ground excuse last time; good chance

runners.name: Sense Of Reason
runners.spotlight: Won on the AW last summer (1m2f) and launched hurdling career with two encouraging fourths, latterly when just over 2l behind Jamada in 18-runner Lingfield maiden (2m, soft) in January; safely held on subsequent handicap debut but still considered here, with the switch to better ground a possible source of improvement.
runners.comment: Safely held on handicap debut but first two hurdles runs were encouraging

runners.name: Crackalackin
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who won on Polytrack in the autumn (1m4f; RPR 78); ran poorly on latest outing five weeks ago but can be very competitive at this level if he takes to hurdling at the first attempt; usual cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Fair Flat racer who has possibilities at this level if he takes to hurdling

runners.name: Somkiyr
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat in France in 2023 but has shown very little over hurdles in Britain this season (five attempts), including off a lowly mark on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won on the Flat in France but has shown very little over hurdles in Britain

races.race_name: Sandcliff Cromer "Grassroots" Maiden Hunters' Chase (For The Turner Trophy)

runners.name: On Lovers Walk
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form under rules when based in Ireland and comfortably won minor point at Paxford (2m4f, good) for new stable a week ago; not sure to be suited by this move back up in trip but still commands respect; sports refitted tongue-tie and first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Fair under rules in Ireland; comfortably won a point on stable debut a week ago

runners.name: Mister Splash
runners.spotlight: Not seen under rules since 2022 but 4-14 in points in the meantime, including success at this track (3m, good) last month and in Higham match race ten days ago; likely contender.
runners.comment: 4-14 in points, including course win last month; likely contender

runners.name: Royal Cave
runners.spotlight: Out of form over hurdles/on the Flat in Ireland last year; caused 16-1 surprise in point at Hutton Rudby (3m, soft) last month, her third start for this stable, but was safely held on next outing; not easy to predict on chase debut.
runners.comment: Surprise winner of a point last month but safely held since; chase debut today

runners.name: Chapelier
runners.spotlight: Teenager who is 0-18 under rules, and has very uninspiring record in points in recent years; remote when third of four on recent seasonal debut at Higham; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Teenager who has very uninspiring record in points in recent years

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Pantile's Gift
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo and again on his handicap debut/reappearance at Chelmsford 18 days ago (1m2f); should be sharper today and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Could step forward from his reappearance fourth; leading contender

runners.name: Raven's View
runners.spotlight: Modest form in his four runs, all here at 6f and 7f; pedigree strongly suggests we will see him in a more favourable light upped to 1m2f.
runners.comment: Modest form in four course runs but this trip should be much more to his liking

runners.name: Sovereign Bright
runners.spotlight: She isn't progressing but she is 10lb lower than for her only other handicap run and this trip should suit on pedigree; not a solid option but cannot be dismissed.
runners.comment: Not progressing but this new, longer trip should suit on paper

runners.name: Project Kinsman
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in three course runs at up to 7f; new trip/switch to handicaps not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in three course runs at up to 7f; not a solid option now handicapping

runners.name: Fuzeyya
runners.spotlight: Her third 2yo run (1m, good to soft; cheekpieces first time) was encouraging; last of five on her seasonal/handicap debut four weeks ago (1m4f, AW); should do better today.
runners.comment: Last of five on her seasonal/handicap but should do better today

runners.name: Due To Shine
runners.spotlight: Finished in the money in her two nursery runs last autumn but she was beaten over 6l on each occasion; improvement necessary to win.
runners.comment: In the frame in two nurseries but beaten over 6l on each occasion; improvement necessary

runners.name: Meritocratic
runners.spotlight: His standout effort at Chelmsford in November (7f) came in a race that hasn't worked out; should appreciate the step up in trip but not obviously well treated for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Up in trip for his handicap debut so could well improve; he needs to off this mark though

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap but he has run well in all four attempts, most recently missing out by a short-head when dropped to 5f for the first time; stepping back up in trip won't hurt and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but he's left the impression it's only a matter of time; big player

runners.name: Pivotal Terms
runners.spotlight: She has finished down the field in three Tapeta runs for her new stable this year; dropping down the weights but would want to see market support before getting involved.
runners.comment: Yet to shine for current stable; would want to see market support before getting involved

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Regularly placed prior to her win in a 5f handicap (cheekpieces first time) in February; creditable efforts since and she holds each-way claims once again.
runners.comment: 5f win here in February; solid efforts in defeat since; each-way claims again

runners.name: Cosmic Clarets
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his first two starts, both over 6f on turf; faced some stiff tasks afterwards and came up well short; gelded since last seen; down in class on AW debut; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Promising start to 2yo season but form dropped off; gelded; down in class for reappearance

runners.name: Fille Unique
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 5f events, including here, as a 2yo; unplaced in two handicap runs but she may have needed it on last month's reappearance and this is a drop in class.
runners.comment: Drops in class with her reappearance behind her; not fully exposed

runners.name: Carnation Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f seller at York last summer (good to firm); well short of that level in two subsequent runs last year; enough to prove back from 209 days off.
runners.comment: Chance if reproducing form of last summer's York win but that was a standout performance

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: Two handicap runs have resulted in heavy defeats, the latest at Pontefract last week (had excuses); not solid but his best run did come at this track (7f).
runners.comment: Well beaten in two handicap attempts; best run came here (7f) but others look safer

runners.name: Forever Twenty
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her three runs in novice company at Southwell (7f-1m); not obviously well treated but she has a new trip to tackle today and her stable is in good form.
runners.comment: Improvement essential now handicapping but it's not out of the question; yard in form

runners.name: Northern Tempest
runners.spotlight: Improved effort at the third attempt when a close fourth of nine in a 5f novice here in November; back up to 6f for his handicap debut; unexposed; stable also runs Brain Freeze.
runners.comment: Best 2yo run came here (5f) when last seen in November; may do better now handicapping
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods

runners.name: Anorah Unleashed
runners.spotlight: Creditable third on her return to action at Kempton (6f) 12 days ago; improvement required to win off this mark but this stiffer test at the trip should suit her.
runners.comment: Fair 3rd at Kempton 12 days ago; this stiffer test at the trip should suit; not dismissed

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to nurseries last autumn; makes her seasonal/AW debut off a reduced mark; others look safer.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicapping last autumn; vulnerable on seasonal/AW debut

runners.name: Stromness
runners.spotlight: Three course runs this winter haven't been devoid of promise and he should find Class 6 handicaps more to his liking; starts handicap career in a competitive race though.
runners.comment: More realistic chance now handicapping but this looks a competitive race

runners.name: Thornaby Annie
runners.spotlight: Two solid efforts in C&D nurseries last October; needs more to defy this mark and she has a six-month absence to contend with.
runners.comment: Two solid runs in C&D nurseries last October; needs more to take this after six months off

runners.name: Taaklam
runners.spotlight: Finished 2l in front of Thornaby Annie in a C&D nursery last October and reopposes on the same terms; sold out of Ed Walker's for 22,000gns later that month; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Third over C&D on final run for Ed Walker in October; sold 22,000gns since; interesting

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noelan Star
