runners.spotlight: Striking debut effort here (1m) in December, coming home a long way clear of a subsequent winner; has a penalty to shoulder and a shorter trip to contend with but his claims are still obvious.
runners.comment: Came home 8.5l in front of a next-time-out winner on debut here (1m); leading claims

runners.name: Viper
runners.spotlight: Market springer on his debut at Doncaster 15 days ago (7f, good to soft) and he shaped nicely amidst evident inexperience; this isn't so competitive and he is open to improvement; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Shaped quite nicely on his stable debut at Doncaster two weeks ago; this looks easier

runners.name: Record Day
runners.spotlight: Finished 1l and one place behind stablemate Viper in a Doncaster maiden (7f, good to soft) 15 days ago; may struggle to reverse those placings.
runners.comment: Finished 1l and one place behind Viper on debut; may struggle to reverse placings

runners.name: Horu Kanu
runners.spotlight: Three runs last summer, showing best form in one attempt over 7f; gelded since last seen; more appealing than many in here.
runners.comment: Pick of his three 2yo runs came over this trip; gelded since last seen; each-way shout

runners.name: Leelawadee
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Ozymandis (7f/1m AW; RPR 102), Spring Bloom (5f/6f; 93) and May Blossom (5f/6f; 88); dam 5f winner (82); stable's newcomers usually better for a run.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners out of a winning sprinter; perhaps a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Tzarmaggedon
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; closely related to 6f winner Chief Mankato (RPR 92); dam 6f 2yo winner (78); should have a future but perhaps one for later on.
runners.comment: Closely related to 6f winner Chief Mankato (RPR 92); bred to have a future; check betting
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Eagle Thunder
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a 1m maiden at Kempton on debut 19 days ago (caught wide); not easily recommended on the back of that.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a 1m maiden at Kempton last month; should do much better at some point

runners.name: Billyjoegold
runners.spotlight: From a family that has served connections well and he ran a fair race to finish third of six on his C&D debut last month; this race demands more of him.
runners.comment: Ran okay on last month's C&D debut but others look more likely candidates today

runners.name: Balmoral Boy
runners.spotlight: Picked up third-place prize money on his debut here last month (1m) but he was beaten 23l at the finish; big step forward required.
runners.comment: Beaten 23l into third on debut last month (1m); a big step forward is required

runners.name: Wee Dresser
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and ran to just a modest level in a C&D maiden 32 days ago.
runners.comment: 150-1 and ran to just a modest level in a C&D maiden 32 days ago

runners.name: Whernside
runners.spotlight: Prominent in the market but ran to a modest level when seventh of ten in a 1m novice here in November; dropping in trip may help; stable also runs Noelan Star.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market billing over 1m here in November; stable also runs Noelean Star
runners.owner: Unique Retreats Stays Ltd And Partner

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: William Dewhirst
runners.spotlight: In fine form in the 2024/25 AW season; absent for 371 days prior to a low-key return last month; may need a few more pounds off his back before he is of renewed interest.
runners.comment: Fine form in the 2024/25 AW season; well beaten after an absence last month; vulnerable

runners.name: Evening Saigon
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; created a positive impression when winning a 6f handicap at Chelmsford in October and his subsequent defeat at Doncaster is easily excused on account of the heavy ground; more to come this year and he's a strong candidate.
runners.comment: Unexposed; good win a Chelmsford in October; ground excuse latest; leading contender

runners.name: Grant Wood
runners.spotlight: Began the season with two C&D wins, the second an impressive one; only fourth at Wolverhampton last time but he was putting in good late work and that form has been well advertised since; another 4lb higher today but the return to Newcastle is in his favour.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this year; fourth at Wolverhampton latest (strong form); solid contender

runners.name: Paddy's Day
runners.spotlight: Won six 5f handicaps here in 2025; only 1lb higher than for the latest of them but he is rarely seen over 6f and connections now turn to a tongue tie for the first time.
runners.comment: Six 5f wins here in 2025; rarely seen at 6f nowadays; tongue tie now reached for

runners.name: Wicket Keeper
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf last summer for Ed Bethell; 33-1 for last month's stable debut but he ran a fine race, fourth past the post but doing best of the far-side group; solid claims off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good fourth (best of far-side group) on stable debut 17 days ago; solid claims

runners.name: Albasheer
runners.spotlight: Second run back from a long break (C&D) was better than the first; on a good mark if able to take another step forward.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark; second run back from a long absence was encouraging; not ruled out

runners.name: Cajetan
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower than when third on his stable debut in February; struggled since and connections now reach for blinkers; not solid but he's more than capable on his day.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but on a dangerous mark and he is now tried in new headgear

runners.name: Toyotomi
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his form in France; shaped quite nicely when fifth in a 6f handicap at Goodwood when last seen in October; gelded the following day; appeals as the  type to win a nice prize this year.
runners.comment: Strong French form; promising Goodwood run when last seen; gelded since; interesting

runners.name: The Caltonian
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to record a decisive C&D success in January; ran no sort of race over 5f here in February, always behind after a slow start; better than that and no surprise to see him rebound.
runners.comment: C&D win in January brings him right into it; subsequent 5f run was a shocker
runners.owner: John Murphy & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Smart sprint handicapper on his day and although he went winless in 2025, he was second in the Stewards' Cup off an 8lb higher mark; likely to win races soon but back from a break isn't usually the time to catch him.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but his record fresh doesn't suggest this will be his day

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eddaari
runners.spotlight: Promise in two turf runs in the autumn and also ran well when second over C&D in November; sets the standard and likely to make a bold bid back from 137 days off.
runners.comment: Three encouraging 2yo runs, including C&D; sets the standard and should make a bold bid

runners.name: Showcasing Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two runs last month but only three ran on debut and he was beaten 14l over C&D last time; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two runs last month but neither piece of form is especially compelling

runners.name: Sovereign Bay
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D on his debut in February; failed to progress when third of five at Lingfield later that month; hooded for the first time; capable of better.
runners.comment: 2nd over C&D on debut; no progress on second start; now tried in a hood; still early days

runners.name: Too Darn Spicy
runners.spotlight: Hint of promise in two course runs last month but she will need a good deal more to take this.
runners.comment: Some promise in two course runs last month but she needs a good step forward to take this

runners.name: Far Far Out
runners.spotlight: First foal of a French Listed-placed 1m4f winner (RRP 99), herself a sister to 1m6f Listed winner Ghostwatch; looks the part on paper and he needs a close market check.
runners.comment: Dam a well-related 1m4f winner in France (RPR 99); looks the part on paper; check betting

runners.name: Heathers Feather
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns 3yo; fourth foal; closely related to 7f 2yo winner Courageous Knight (RPR 94); dam French 1m1f winner (92), half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Ibn Khaldun; looks the part on paper but stable wouldn't be renowned for winning newcomers.
runners.comment: 1,000gns 3yo; useful pedigree but yard not renowned for winning newcomers

runners.name: Alembic
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two 1m novice runs this spring; middle-distance handicaps more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Down the field in two 1m runs this spring; middle-distance handicaps beckon

runners.name: Master Of Entropy
runners.spotlight: Fetched 180,000euros at the breeze-up sales last May and he was sent off a well-backed second favourite for his Haydock debut last September (7f, good to soft); dropped right away on that occasion and a subsequent wind op suggests that wasn't his running; interesting to see if his supporters return.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market billing on 2yo debut; wind op since; better may be forthcoming

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Barleybrown
runners.spotlight: Won four times over C&D in 2024 and took minor honours over C&D in his latest three outings; probably a leading player.
runners.comment: Minor honours over C&D in his latest three outings; probably a leading player

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: Last five wins were here, latest over C&D in January and he's 1lb lower today; this 12yo has been operating at a lower level in recent starts, though.
runners.comment: 12yo; C&D win in January; 1lb lower today but operating at a lower level in recent starts

runners.name: Falaise Blanc
runners.spotlight: Blinkers helped in the second half of last year, including with a 1m2f win here (made all); however, he needs to leave last month's reappearance effort way behind him.
runners.comment: 1m2f win here (made all) last year; needs to leave reappearance effort way behind him

runners.name: Lady Delila
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who is 0-17 overall and 0-5 for new yard but she's been competitive in classified events on last three outings; penultimate one was over C&D, before she closed to within a neck at Wolverhampton (9.4f) on latest; considered back in this handicap with first-time visor replacing her occasional hood/cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-17 overall but competitive in classifieds on last three outings; new headgear

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Rare winner (2-37) but runner-up three times in 2026, including over C&D, and two of those were close calls; not disgraced on latest outing and has place claims again.
runners.comment: Rare winner (2-37) but runner-up three times in 2026, including over C&D

runners.name: Sassy Glory
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win over C&D in September was her 23rd career start; not so hot since, so it's probably best to look elsewhere for win purposes.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win over C&D in September was her 23rd career start; not so hot since
runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club

runners.name: Mercurius Power
runners.spotlight: Regular 1m placings here and at Southwell since September; form dipped on latest outing, however, and there's been no win since August 2024.
runners.comment: Regular 1m placings here and at Southwell since September; form dipped on latest outing

runners.name: Teggy Lasso
runners.spotlight: Has rather gone the wrong way on Tapeta in his three handicaps, never dangerous when fitted with cheekpieces over 1m last time; now tongue tied as well; lightly raced and competitively handicapped on his best form.
runners.comment: Now tongue tied; lightly raced and competitively handicapped on his best form
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Wendy O'Leary

runners.name: Berkshire Phantom
runners.spotlight: His heyday was two winters back but he still won last July (1m1f on turf) and is often thereabouts, including on Tapeta; not totally ruled out despite the 106-day absence.
runners.comment: Won last July (turf) and is often thereabouts; off 106 days but not totally ruled out

runners.name: Rocket Rod
runners.spotlight: Well suited by Newcastle but absent for almost two years before his last of ten here (50-1) in February; probably best watched again.
runners.comment: Absent for almost two years before his last of ten here (50-1) in February

runners.name: The Cookstown Cafu
runners.spotlight: Way down the weights now but hasn't won since October 2024 (C&D) and had a quiet end to last season.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since October 2024 (C&D) and had a quiet end to last season

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Darlo Pride
runners.spotlight: Having slipped in the weights he returned to form with a close third at Southwell five weeks ago; won't be inconvenienced by this stiffer test and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Back to form with a close 3rd at Southwell last month; C&D winner; one to take seriously
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods

runners.name: Dc Cogent
runners.spotlight: His only win came over C&D but that was a maiden in which he was a red-hot favourite; three solid efforts in 6f handicaps this year and he can't be dismissed back a 5f.
runners.comment: C&D winner; three solid runs over 6f this year; not dismissed back at 5f
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Herakles
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come over C&D; not beaten far at Southwell on his last two starts; dropped another 1lb and he's a leading contender back at this track; stable runs three here.
runners.comment: Three of his four wins have come over C&D; arrives in good form; leading contender

runners.name: Rock Of England
runners.spotlight: Should come on for last month's Southwell reappearance; returning to a stiff 5f no bad thing either and it will be interesting to see how he fares in the betting with Paul Midgley running three here.
runners.comment: Should come on for last month's reappearance; return to 5f a plus too; stable run three

runners.name: Ganesha
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over C&D in December; less good since and he needs his 66-day break to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice over C&D in December; fair mark but not at best in last three starts

runners.name: Blue Force
runners.spotlight: Four 5f wins on Tapeta, two of them at this track; attracted support and returned to form with a closing third at Wolverhampton nine days ago; 1lb lower today; considered.
runners.comment: Finished race off well for 3rd at Wolverhampton nine days ago; 1lb lower today; considered
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Zuffolo
runners.spotlight: Made all in a first-time visor over C&D last September; ended 2025 quietly and although he is only 1lb higher than for his win, he returns from a break with a point to prove.
runners.comment: C&D win off 1lb lower last September; ended 2025 quietly; opposable back from a break
runners.owner: R R D Saunders And Partner

runners.name: Hover On The Wind
runners.spotlight: Beat Ganesha over C&D in December off a 2lb lower mark; unplaced on his next two starts but he returned to form when a fast-finishing third at Southwell last month, a race in which he was short of room at a crucial stage; races off the same mark and he is one of three interesting contenders for the stable.
runners.comment: C&D win in December and an unlucky third at Southwell last month; stable runs three here

runners.name: Bonito Cavalo
runners.spotlight: His penultimate start (C&D), his first after a wind op, offered hope he might be ready to exploit his much-reduced mark; less good over 6f last month and now steps back up in class.
runners.comment: Penultimate run was more promising but backward step latest; others look safer

runners.name: Brian The Snail
runners.spotlight: Veteran who never featured in a 6f handicap at Wolverhampton nine days ago; drops back to 5f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Veteran who never featured at Wolverhampton nine days ago; opposable back at 5f
